title: Memoria Navis
expandedDescription: Memoria Navis is a story-driven and meditative game that uses brainwaves to enhance the experience.

I was part of a six-person student team working for our client, Roel Heremans. His NeuroRight Arcades used EEG brain-computer interfaces called Enophones to create reflective arcade experiences about neurotechnology.

Our task was to build a game around the Enophones while keeping that reflective tone. We made a game where the player controls a spaceship that was once guided by a wiped human consciousness, then helps the ship recover memories through thought and interaction.
I was one of two programmers on the team. A major part of my work was reading and processing EEG signals through BrainFlow so the brain-computer interface could influence gameplay.
One of my biggest systems was the meditation orb. During meditation, the game reads EEG data and uses it to affect the orb's color, size, turbulence, cohesion, and related parameters.
I also worked on ship movement, spark AI behavior with blended steering, and systems that spawn and despawn asteroids or debris to create the feeling of moving through an endless field.
